description: In a coffee shop, Chandler leans over the back of an orange sofa. "For the rest of my life," he says.
Monica, seated on the sofa, turns her head toward Chandler.
Monica turns forward, gesturing with her hands. "I mean, I can't break up with him. I'm the one who made him quit drinking. He's dull because of me."
Phoebe stands nearby in a patterned top. "All right, don't say that. He's probably"
Phoebe gestures with one hand. "always been dull. You just, you know, set it free."
Chandler stands up and leans on the sofa. A man wearing a black leather jacket, Russ, enters the coffee shop in the background.
Chandler looks toward the man and says, "Hey, Ross."
Russ waves one hand and makes a brief "bah" sound, then touches his forehead.
Rachel stands near Russ holding a cloth. "Hi, Russ. I've just got two more tables to clean, and then we'll go."
Russ nods and gestures. "Okay. Okay. I'll just sit here and chat with your"
Russ walks past Rachel and approaches Phoebe.
Russ stops in front of Phoebe and finishes, "friend type people."
Russ turns and walks away. Phoebe maintains her gaze on him, then turns her head toward Rachel.
Phoebe walks up to Rachel. "Rachel?" Rachel responds, "Yeah."
Phoebe says, "Hi."
Phoebe leans in slightly. "Okay, so you know what you're doing, right?"
Rachel looks at Phoebe.
Rachel holds a tray. "Waitressing?"
Phoebe shakes her head slightly. "Well, yeah, but no."
Phoebe continues, "I mean,"
Phoebe shifts her posture.
Phoebe tilts her head. "doesn't Russ just remind you of someone?"
Russ is seen sitting at a table in the background, reading a menu.
Rachel looks at Phoebe. "Bob Saget." Phoebe replies, "Oh, yeah. Oh, no."
Ross, wearing a brown jacket, enters the coffee shop and walks toward Phoebe. Phoebe spots him and inhales sharply.
Ross stops and says, "Hi." Phoebe covers her mouth with her hand and replies, "Oh, my."
Ross looks at Phoebe, his eyebrows drawing together.
Ross asks, "What's wrong?"
Monica stands up from the sofa.
Monica steps between Ross and Phoebe. "Okay, she's just upset because she buttered a spider into her toast this morning."
Ross looks back and forth between Monica and Phoebe.
Ross says, "All right,"
Ross walks past them. Chandler approaches from the sofa.
Chandler touches Phoebe's shoulders. "Mr. Phoebe, this is going to be okay."
Chandler steps past Phoebe.
Chandler gestures toward Ross. "Ross. Ross. Ross."
Chandler points again. "Ross."
Ross turns around as Russ stands up. They end up facing each other.
Ross looks at Russ. "Hi."
Russ looks back at Ross.
Russ asks, "Are you a friend of?" Ross nods, "Yes. Yes, I am. Are you a friend of Rachel's?"
Russ answers, "Actually, I'm"
Russ continues, "kind of"
Ross and Russ maintain eye contact.
Russ gestures slightly. "a date type thing"
Ross's gaze remains fixed on Russ.
Russ adds, "of Rachel."
Ross raises his eyebrows.
Ross asks, "A date?"
Russ replies, "Yeah, I'm her"
Ross narrows his eyes and points a finger at Russ.
Ross says, "oh, you're the"
Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe sit together on the sofa.
Chandler says to them, "You know, this is actually good because if we ever lose Ross, we have a spare."
Ross points a rolled-up newspaper at Russ. "Oh, you are the"
Ross lowers the newspaper slightly.
Russ finishes, "paleontologist."
Ross points at him again. "Yes, I am. And you are a"
Russ looks at him.
Russ answers, "periodontist."
Monica turns to her friends. "See, now they're as different as night and"
Chandler looks forward.
Chandler says, "later that night."
Russ gestures. "Well, I am going to get a beverage. It was nice meeting you."
Ross nods. "Ditto."
Russ turns and walks toward the counter. Ross exhales audibly and looks down.
Rachel hands a receipt to Russ at the counter. Ross walks over to them.
Ross stands near Rachel. "Well, I met Russ."
Rachel turns her attention to Ross.
Ross says, "Hey. I didn't know we were seeing other people."
Rachel answers, "Well, we're not seeing each other."
Ross looks down and nods slightly.
Ross gestures with one hand. "Well, for your information, there's a woman at the museum who's curator of moths and other"
Ross makes a fluttering motion with his hands.
Ross finishes the fluttering gesture and points to his chest. "winged things who's let it be known that she is drawn to me much like a."
Ross rubs his chest briefly. Rachel keeps her gaze on him.
Ross says, "Well, so far I've been keeping her at bay, but if this is the deal"
Rachel hands a small bag to Russ. "well, yeah, this is the deal." Ross replies, "Okay. Well, have a nice evening." Rachel says, "Thank you."
Russ stands next to Rachel.
Rachel looks at Russ. "Russ, you ready?"
Russ nods and says, "Yeah," as they begin to leave.
In a coffee shop, Ross sits on a couch holding a folded newspaper. Monica, Phoebe, and Chandler sit on an adjacent couch and chair. Rachel and Russ walk toward the exit.
Rachel waves and says, "Bye." Ross replies, "Bye."
Ross watches them leave, then leans forward, turning his attention to Monica, Phoebe, and Chandler.
Ross gestures with his newspaper and asks, "She's dating?"
Ross leans closer toward the group, continuing to gesture.
Chandler says, "She's dating. Yes, but did you see who she was dating?"
Ross frowns and asks, "What do you mean?"
Monica leans forward, looking directly at Ross.
Monica asks, "Do you not see it?" Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "See what? I don't know what she sees in that goober"
Ross waves his hand dismissively toward the door.
Ross continues, gesturing with his hand and the newspaper, "takes him, what, like, I don't know, hello? A week to get out a sentence?"
Chandler tilts his head slightly, watching Ross.
Chandler replies, "Yeah, it's annoying, isn't it?"
Ross points at Chandler and smiles.
Ross says, "Yeah."
The setting changes to a kitchen, where Joey, wearing an apron, pours tomato sauce from a pot into a funnel over a jar.
Numerous jars of red sauce cover the counters. Chandler enters the apartment through the front door.
Chandler says, "Hey."
Joey looks toward Chandler, continuing to hold the pot.
Chandler looks at the numerous sauce jars and says, "Whoa."
Chandler steps further into the kitchen and says, "Whoa."
Joey continues holding the pot as Chandler approaches the counter.
Chandler says, "So I'm guessing you didn't get the part or Italy called and said it was hungry." Joey sets the pot down on the stove.
Joey picks the pot back up.
Joey resumes pouring sauce and says, "The part's mine if I want it." Chandler removes his coat and says, "Oh, my God."
Joey adds, "Yeah, if I'm willing to sleep with the casting lady." Chandler says, "Oh, my"
Chandler finishes, "God," spreading his arms outward.
Joey holds the pot and funnel over the jar.
Joey says, "Ten years I've been waiting for a break like this. Chandler."
Joey continues, gesturing with his free hand, "Ten years. I mean Days of Our Lives."
Joey says, "That's actually on television." Chandler asks, "And so, what are you going to do?"
Joey grabs a blue towel, sets the funnel down, and says, "I guess I could sleep with her. I mean, how can"
Joey finishes, "I do that?" Chandler gestures with his thumb and says, "Well, I've got a pop-up book that told me everything I need to know."
Joey wipes his hands on the towel and says, "I've never slept with someone for a part." Chandler asks, "Well, is she"
Joey hands the blue towel to Chandler, who takes it.
Chandler says, "sorry."
Chandler throws the towel back to Joey.
Chandler asks, "Is she good looking?"
Joey catches the towel and says, "Yeah, she's totally good looking. I mean, if I met her in a bar or something I'd be buying her breakfast"
Joey continues wiping his hands with the towel.
Joey finishes, "after having slept with her."
Chandler points at Joey, who turns away. Both men walk toward the chairs in the living area and sit down.
Chandler sits and says, "You know, maybe this isn't such a big deal, you know? I mean, the way that I see it is you get a great job and you get to have sex."
Joey sits in an armchair, looking at Chandler.
Chandler continues, gesturing slightly, "I mean, throw in a tree and a fat guy and you've got Christmas."
Joey leans back in the chair and sighs heavily.
Joey leans forward, gesturing with one hand, "I just don't think that I want it that way, though."
Joey looks down, then back up at Chandler.
Joey says, "Let's say I do make it, all right? I'm always going to look back and wonder if it was because of my talent or because know"
Joey finishes, gesturing near his waist, "the little general?"
Chandler sits with the blue towel in his hands, looking at Joey.
Chandler asks, "Didn't you used to call it the little major?"
Joey raises his eyebrows slightly.
Joey gestures outward and says, "Yeah, but after Denise DeMarco, I had to promote it."
The setting changes to the exterior of a restaurant at night, with a neon sign reading "Monte's Restaurant." Yellow taxis drive past.
Inside the restaurant, Monica and Fun Bobby sit at a table. A waiter approaches and asks, "Can I get you something from the bar?" Monica looks at Fun Bobby, then says, "Yes, I would like something."
Fun Bobby turns toward the waiter.
Fun Bobby says to the waiter, "No, thank you."
Fun Bobby tells Monica, "If you want a drink, it's okay with me. I've got to get used to it."
Monica replies, waving her hand slightly, "No, really. I wouldn't feel right about it. Just some water."
The waiter nods and walks away.
Fun Bobby says, "So the light went out in my refrigerator." Monica looks up at the departing waiter and calls out, "I'd like a Scotch on the rocks with a twist."
Monica smiles widely at Fun Bobby.
The setting changes back to the coffee shop. Ross, Chandler, and Russ sit on a couch. Chandler looks at Russ on the right and says, "Hey, we're having some fun now, Ross."
Chandler holds a newspaper in his lap.
Chandler points at the newspaper and asks, "Want to do another one?"
Russ points to himself and asks, "Ross?"
Chandler's eyes widen. He looks down at his newspaper.
Chandler says, "Okay."
Chandler continues, "Eleven letters."
Chandler traces the paper with his pencil.
Chandler reads from the newspaper, "Atomic element number 101 ends in ium".
Ross, seated on the left, says, "dysprosium". Russ repeats, "dysprosium,"
Ross says, "mendelevium".
Chandler points his pencil at Ross and says, "and weenie number two has it that's, of course, nine down Nights in White Satin was sung by"
Chandler finishes, "The Moody Blues."
Chandler holds the pencil, looking at the newspaper.
Inside a coffee shop, Rachel leans against the counter holding a towel. Phoebe stands facing her.
Phoebe leans forward slightly and says, "You don't see it? You actually don't see it?"
Rachel asks, "What?" Phoebe replies, "Okay, honey, you're dating Ross."
Rachel gestures with her hands. "No, Phoebes. I'm dating Russ." Phoebe counters, gesturing, "Russ is Ross. Russ, Ross. Steve, sleeve."
Rachel points at Phoebe and says, "Okay, no"
Rachel finishes, "one is named Sleeve."
Phoebe gestures with one hand as Rachel crosses her arms.
Rachel gestures with her hands and asks, "What the hell are you talking about? Other than their names being similar?"
Rachel continues, "I'm sorry, I do not see what you're seeing."

At a sofa in the coffee shop, Ross, Chandler, and Russ sit together. Russ has an appearance nearly identical to Ross. Ross looks at Russ and says, "For your information, it's a card sharp, not a card shark."
Russ replies, "You could not be more wrong."
Ross says, "You could try, but you would not be successful."
Chandler reacts with a subtle facial movement.
Chandler folds his newspaper and stands up. "Okay, I'm going to get some more coffee before the pinching and eye poking begins."
Chandler walks away from the sofa. Ross looks toward Russ.
Russ points at Ross and says, "I know what your problem is." Ross retorts, "Oh, you do, do you?"
Russ says, "You're jealous."
Ross crosses his arms, stammers slightly, and looks away.
Ross asks, "Of what?"
Russ replies, "You're jealous because I'm a real doctor."
Ross smiles and uncrosses his arms.
Ross points at Russ. "Hey, you're a doctor of gums. That's the smallest body part you can major in."
Ross continues, leaning back, "It's like, day one"
Ross continues, "floss. Day two, here's your diploma."
Russ points his finger at Ross and says, "No, let me finish."
Ross leans forward, pointing back. "No, let me finish."
Russ points again. "No, you let me finish."
Ross and Russ stop arguing and turn their heads to look over the back of the sofa as Rachel approaches holding a coffee pot. Ross and Russ simultaneously say, "Hi."
Rachel looks back and forth between Ross and Russ with her eyes widened. She turns and walks away. Audience laughter is audible.
An exterior wide view of a city skyline is shown during the daytime.

The setting changes to an apartment. Ross sits at the kitchen table while Rachel lies back in an armchair. Chandler enters the living area. Rachel asks, "Did Joey say what he was going to do when he left?"
Chandler walks past Rachel holding a small carton. "No, I don't even think he knew. Hey, would you sleep with somebody to get a great"
Chandler stops near the armchair. "job?" Rachel replies, "I don't know."
Rachel looks up at Chandler. "Who would I have to sleep with?" Chandler points to himself and says, "Me."
Rachel smiles, looks away, and then looks back at him. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel asks, "Why would I have to sleep with you?" Chandler gestures with his free hand. "It's my game. You want the job or not?"
Monica enters the apartment carrying a large blue duffel bag.
Chandler says, "Hey." Monica replies, "Morning." Chandler asks, "Where are you going?" Monica places the duffel bag on the kitchen table. "Bobby and I are going away for the weekend. Remember?"
Ross says, "Oh," as Monica walks to the refrigerator.
Monica pulls a clear plastic bag filled with small bottles from the refrigerator. Ross asks, "What? All the bottles of liquor?"
Phoebe, seated at the table, gestures with her hands. "Excuse me, miss? In the event of a water landing can this seat be used as a flotation device?"
Ross looks at Monica while holding a knife and a bagel half.
Ross points his knife toward Monica. "What's going on? Is Bobby drinking?" Monica puts the plastic bag into the duffel bag. "Oh, no, no. This is not for him. This is for me."
Monica continues packing. "That way he's still sober, but I find his"
Monica holds up a small bottle. "stories about shoelaces much more amusing."
Chandler smiles as he holds a bottle. Monica zips the duffel bag closed. Audience laughter is audible.
Two knocks are heard. Rachel rolls her eyes and says, "God, even his knock is boring."
Monica walks to the door carrying the plastic bag of bottles.
Monica opens the door. Fun Bobby stands in the hallway and says, "Hi."
Monica says, "I'll be ready in just a second." Bobby says, "Could I talk to you a minute?"
Monica says, "Sure," and steps into the hallway.
The setting changes to the hallway outside the apartment. Bobby says, "This is really hard for me to say."
Monica places a hand on her chest. "Oh, God. You fell off the wagon." Bobby raises his hands. "Oh, no, it's about you."
Monica asks, "What about me?" Bobby replies, "I..."
Bobby finishes, "think you may have a drinking problem."
Monica looks down at the plastic bag in her hand.
Monica lifts the bag slightly. "What, these? No, these are for cuts and scrapes."
Bobby shakes his head.
Bobby gestures with his hands. "Look, I am just not strong enough to be in a codependent relationship right now, okay?"
Monica looks at him in silence.
Monica says softly, "Oh, shoot."
Monica lowers her gaze, then looks back up at Bobby.
Bobby says, "Well, anyway, I hope we can be friends."
Monica smiles slightly and says, "Okay."
Monica and Bobby embrace in the hallway.
In a hallway outside an apartment door, Monica stands with Fun Bobby.
Fun Bobby says, "Take care," and walks away down the hall. Monica says, "Okay. You too. Bye."
Monica opens the door and enters an apartment kitchen holding a clear plastic bag. Chandler, Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel sit around a dining table.
Rachel asks, "What happened?"
Monica looks toward the table.
Monica stops, holding the bag, and says, "Well, we kind of broke up."
The others at the table react softly, "Oh," as Ross gestures toward the table.
The group stands up or turns toward Monica as she approaches the table. Chandler reaches out and takes the plastic bag from her.
Monica asks, "Does anybody want these?" Chandler pulls a small bottle from the bag and says, "I'll take one."
Chandler examines the small bottle.
Holding the small bottle up between his thumb and forefinger, Chandler says, "Sometimes I like to hold stuff like this and pretend I'm a giant."
Chandler sets the bottle down, and the others sit back down as audience laughter is heard.
The apartment door opens and Joey enters.
Ross says, "Hey, how's the callback going?" Joey replies, "It was unbelievable. I walked in there, and she was all over me." Rachel asks, "So, what'd you do?" Joey answers, "Well,"

Gesturing with his hands, Joey continues, "I couldn't do it. I told her I didn't want to get the part that way. Wait, wait. But then after I left her office she caught up with" The group cheers and claps as he speaks.
He continues, "me at the elevator and offered me an even bigger part."
Joey grins.
Smiling widely, he announces loudly, "So you are now looking at Dr. Drake Ramoray, neurosurgeon, recurring in at least four"
He pumps his fists in the air and shouts, "episodes." The group and the audience cheer and clap loudly.
He lowers his arms, still smiling.
Pointing toward the hallway, he says, "All right, I gotta go shower," and walks off.
Monica claps her hands, smiling, as the others sit. Joey returns to grab a blue bag from the table, then leaves again.
On-screen text reading "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE" appears over the group, then the screen fades to black.
Inside a coffee shop, Russ walks past tables toward a couch where Phoebe and Chandler are sitting.
Phoebe and Chandler look up. Russ stops near the couch.
Looking down slightly, Russ says, "Rachel dumped me." Chandler replies, "Yeah, I'm sorry, man." Russ continues, "All she said was that I reminded her too much of somebody. Do you have any idea"
Russ looks toward Chandler.
He asks, "who she's talking about?"
Chandler glances at Phoebe.
Chandler looks back and says, "Oh, I do. It's Bob Saget."
Russ looks confused as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler adds, "She hates him."
Russ nods slightly. Julie approaches the couch from behind.
Julie waves and says, "Hey,"
The group turns to look at Julie.
Phoebe says, "Julie. Hey. How are you?"
Julie says, "I don't know."
Julie stops behind the couch and says, "I mean, it's definitely weird not being with Ross. But I guess I'm doing okay."
Looking down, she continues, "Actually, I've got some of his stuff that he"
On-screen text credits executive story editors. Julie stops speaking as she notices Russ standing nearby.
She stares at him as more on-screen text appears. Russ looks back at her, their gaze sustained, and audience laughter is heard.

Julie asks him softly, "Have we met?" Russ replies, "I don't think so." Julie says, "He looks so familiar," keeping her eyes on him. On-screen text includes "guest starring Vincent Ventresca as Fun Bobby".
On-screen text continues, including "and Snaro as Russ". Russ tilts his head.
Russ says softly, "Maybe"
Russ looks into Julie's eyes, and she maintains eye contact.
Russ finishes, "we could talk about it over a cup of coffee?" Julie shakes her head slightly and says, "You know what, I'm sick of coffee. Let's go get us some juice."
Russ smiles, keeping his gaze on Julie.
Russ says, "All right."
They turn to leave together.
Phoebe and Chandler wave and say, "Bye."
Phoebe and Chandler watch them walk away, looking bewildered. On-screen text credits production staff.
The screen fades to black.
The screen remains completely black, holding in silence until the segment ends.
